findings.summary: KEYSW keyboard switches misclassified as 'relay' (28 instances); key_matrices reports switches_on_matrix=0 due to relay misclassification; I2C SCL net incorrectly mapped to ProMicro pin 15 (GND), S...

correct.description: row0-row3 and col0-col6 nets correctly enumerated. 28 diodes on matrix correctly counted. Detection method 'net_name' is appropriate for this design.
BOM quantities match the schematic source. The 28 key switches and 28 diodes align with the 4x7 matrix. Connector counts are reasonable.

R2 is correctly identified as the SCL pull-up at 4.7kΩ to VCC. The sdaPM net lacking a pull-up is also correctly noted (it routes through SJ1 solder jumper to 'sda' which has its own pull-up on the expander side).

incorrect.description: Components with value 'KEYSW' and lib_id 'ErgoTravel-rescue:KEYSW' are typed as 'relay' in both component_types stats and per-component type fields. They are mechanical key switches (footprint SW_MX_reversible), not relays. Should be 'switch'. This cascades into key_matrices reporting switches_on_matrix=0 because no 'switch'-typed components are found on the matrix nets, even though K1-K28 are all connected to col/row nets.

Because KEYSW parts are classified as 'relay', the key matrix detector finds 0 switches on the matrix (only diodes are found). The actual 28 key switches (K1-K28) are all connected to col nets. switches_on_matrix should be 28.

The nets 'scl' and 'sdaPM' appear in the bus_analysis and erc_warnings as being connected to U1 pins 15 and 16 respectively, both named 'GND'. This is a net-to-pin resolution error in the legacy KiCad 5 parser — global labels placed on wires near GND pins are being attributed to those GND pins rather than the correct MCU I/O pins. The ProMicro's actual I2C pins (PD1/PD0 or similar) should be the I2C signals.

The design_observation 'reset_pin' says U1 RST pin is on net 'col0' with no pull-up. In this keyboard design the hardware RST pin IS wired to col0 (an unusual but intentional choice for keyboard firmware use). However, the separate 'RESET' net (connected to PF5/SW1/SW2) is the firmware-reset mechanism and is not flagged. The observation is technically accurate about the wire connectivity but contextually misleading — this is intentional in QMK-based keyboard designs that use RST as a matrix column.

missed.description: The schematic has nets named SCK, MOSI, MISO routed from U1 to the ICSP header J4 (Conn_01x06). The bus_analysis SPI list is empty. These are clearly SPI/ICSP signals by name and should be detected.

findings.summary: Same KEYSW→relay misclassification and switches_on_matrix=0 issue as PCB/ErgoTravel.sch

incorrect.description: Choc variant shows identical patterns: 27 KEYSW classified as relay, switches_on_matrix=0, SCL/SDA pins mapped to GND pins. This confirms the issues are systematic across both files.
